title: What the Terminal Is
summary: Understand the terminal as a place to type commands and see the computer respond.

sketchBlocks.title: Introduction to the Terminal
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
The terminal is a powerful tool that allows you to interact with your computer using text commands. Unlike graphical interfaces, the terminal provides a direct way to execute commands and see immediate results.

### Example

```bash
pwd
```

This command prints the current working directory, showing you where you are in the file system.

Try it yourself: Open your terminal and type `pwd` to see your current directory.

sketchBlocks.title: Navigating the File System
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
In the terminal, you can navigate through directories using commands like `cd`, `ls`, and `pwd`.

- `pwd`: Prints the current directory.
- `ls`: Lists files and directories in the current directory.
- `cd`: Changes the current directory.

### Example

```bash
cd /home
ls
pwd
```

This sequence changes the directory to `/home`, lists its contents, and prints the current directory.

Try this: Use `cd` to navigate to a different directory and use `ls` to list its contents.

quizDraft.title: Using pwd to Find Your Location
quizDraft.prompt: Use the terminal to find out your current directory using the `pwd` command.
quizDraft.hint: Type `pwd` in the terminal to see your current directory.

help.concept: The `pwd` command prints the current working directory.
help.hint_1: Use `pwd` to display your current location in the file system.

quizDraft.title: Listing Files with ls
quizDraft.prompt: Use the `ls` command to list all files and directories in the current directory.
quizDraft.hint: Type `ls` to see the contents of the current directory.

help.concept: The `ls` command lists files and directories in the current directory.
help.hint_1: Use `ls` to view the contents of your current directory.

quizDraft.title: Changing Directories with cd
quizDraft.prompt: Use the `cd` command to change to the `/home` directory.
quizDraft.template: A file-writing code exercise should also ____ so automated tests can check it.
quizDraft.hint: Type `cd /home` to change to the `/home` directory.

help.concept: The `cd` command changes the current directory.
help.hint_1: Use `cd` followed by the directory path to change directories.
help.hint_2: Type `cd /home` and press Enter to navigate to `/home`.
